digest.component_or_area: release pipeline build/provenance (Hatchling build hook + release workflow)
propositions: The published wheel is built from the extracted sdist (no .git present) so the provenance hook bakes COMMIT = None while only the sdist retains the real commit SHA.
The release artifact and the CI-tested code come from different build paths, and the real artifact is only smoke-tested post-publication when PyPI immutability makes recovery costly.

Adopt a build-once pattern: build wheel and sdist once, upload one named bundle with SHA256SUMS, test the exact artifacts in isolated jobs, and publish the same bytes after hash verification without rebuilding.

Fix hatch_build.py initialize to resolve COMMIT via four-step precedence — REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT env var, preserve existing non-null COMMIT (install-from-sdist), git rev-parse --short HEAD, else None — failing only when the env var is set but empty.
Ship _build_info.py in both wheel and sdist via build_data force_include so the preserve-existing path has a baked SHA to keep through a rebuild-from-sdist.

Use short SHA as the canonical commit format to stay consistent with signing.py, and extend test_release_workflow_pins.py and test_attestation_signing.py with static and end-to-end checks pinning the fail-fast and git-fallback branches.
